prs.title: [WIP] Add TRITON_MLA_SPARSE backend for SM80 sparse MLA support
prs.author: haosdent
prs.body_head:
## Purpose

Closes #38006. Enables sparse MLA models (GLM-5, DeepSeek-V3.2) on SM80 (A100/A800) and SM121 (GB10/DGX Spark), where DeepGEMM / FlashMLA-Sparse / FlashInfer-MLA-Sparse are unavailable.

## Changes

1. **Dispatch guard.** `is_deep_gemm_supported()` (SM90+ check) replaces `has_deep_gemm()` in `sparse_attn_indexer.py` / `indexer.py`. Stops DeepGEMM SM90+ kernels from being invoked on SM80/SM121.
2. **Triton `fp8_mqa_logits` for the indexer.** `mqa_logits_triton.py` reproduces DeepGEMM's prefill + paged MQA logits using a uint8/int bit-trick (`_decode_e4m3fn`) so the kernel compiles on SM80 without `tl.float8e4nv`.
3. **`TRITON_MLA_SPARSE` backend.** `triton_sparse_mla_kernel.py` adds a split-KV decode path with N-way online-softmax merge, plus a single-pass fast path. Autotune is warmed at backend init. Masked-out sentinel is `-1e30` (not `-inf`) to avoid `NaN` from `(-inf) − (-inf)` on short prefills.
4. **Cudagraph support.** `TritonMLASparseMetadataBuilder` advertises `AttentionCGSupport.UNIFORM_BATCH`; flips A100 TP=8 back to `FULL_AND_PIECEWISE`.
5. **Persistent topk race fix** (`csrc/topk.cu`, `persistent_topk.cuh`). Workspace `cudaMemsetAsync` before each launch. The kernel's multi-CTA radix barrier was being initialized in CTA 0 behind a CTA-local `__syncthreads`, which doesn't grid-sync; with workspace reused across launches, CTAs 1..N-1 incremented stale counters before CTA 0's init wrote 0 — deadlocked decode at runtime `seq_len > 32768` (NCCL `_ALLGATHER_BASE` watchdog).
6. **MXFP4 link stubs.** `mxfp4_experts_quant` / `silu_and_mul_mxfp4_experts_quant` stubs in `nvfp4_quant_entry.cu`. `mxfp4_experts_quant.cu` is gated to SM 10.x in CMake but `torch_bindings.cpp` references the symbols unconditionally, breaking source builds on SM 8.x. `VLLM_USE_PRECOMPILED=1` wheels aren't affected — upstream CI builds with all archs in `CUDA_ARCHS`, so the precompiled .so always carries the symbol.

## Benchmarks

8×A100 SXM TP=8, `lukealonso/GLM-5.1-NVFP4`,

prs.title: [New Model][ROCm] Add AMD support for DeepSeek V4
prs.author: whx-sjtu
prs.body_head:
## Purpose
This PR is based on #40760 and further supports DeepSeek V4 for AMD.

## Test Plan

## Test Result
Launch command:
```
max_num_seqs=512
max_num_batched_tokens=8192
tensor_parallel_size=4
export VLLM_TORCH_PROFILER_DIR="/app/vllm_profile"
export HF_HOME=/data/huggingface-cache
export VLLM_ROCM_USE_AITER=1
export VLLM_ROCM_USE_AITER_LINEAR=1
export VLLM_DSV4_WO_A_FP8=0

unset FLATMM_HIP_CLANG_PATH
MODEL=deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Flash

vllm serve ${MODEL} \
    --host localhost \
    --port 8000 \
    --dtype auto \
    --tensor-parallel-size ${tensor_parallel_size} \
    --max-num-seqs ${max_num_seqs} \
    --distributed-executor-backend mp \
    --trust-remote-code \
    --profiler-config '{"profiler": "torch", "torch_profiler_dir": "./vllm_profile"}' \
    --gpu-memory-utilization 0.40 \
    --moe-backend "triton_unfused" \
    --enforce-eager \
    --tokenizer-mode "deepseek_v4" \
    --async-scheduling
```
curl result:
```
curl -s http://localhost:8000/v1/completions -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{
  "prompt": "Write me a poem about AMD and Deepseek",
  "model": "/home/models/DeepSeek-V4-Flash",
  "max_tokens": 100,
  "temperature": 0.0
}'

prs.title: [CI][AMD]BugFix] Fix deadlock occuring in test_moe_layer
prs.author: rasmith
prs.body_head:
## Purpose
`test_moe_layer` is hitting a deadlock on MI300 during execution of the` test_moe_layer::test_moe_layer` tests which spawn multiple workers for these tests which use `TP=2,4`. There is a `WeakValueDictionary` [here](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/blob/main/vllm/distributed/device_communicators/base_device_communicator.py#L15) which caches `DeepEP` buffers [here](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/blob/main/vllm/distributed/device_communicators/all2all.py#L245) and when garbage collection runs, these weak references can be non-deterministically collected.  The `DeepEP` buffer's that were collected will result in a no-op when they are [called](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/blob/main/vllm/distributed/device_communicators/base_device_communicator.py#L25), while those that were not collected will wait indefinitely.  This results in the parallel workers with buffers that weren't collected deadlocking, since they are unable to make progress, and eventually the following error message will be seen:

`DeepEP error: CPU recv timeout`

This PR inserts a barrier at the finally clause, so that after each test, all workers will clear their caches before proceeding to the next test, which allows every worker to make progress, avoiding any deadlock.
## Test Plan
` pytest -sv kernels/moe/test_moe_layer.py::test_moe_layer`
## Test Result
No deadlocks result.

` 12 passed, 20 skipped, 17 warnings in 349.84s`


---
<details>
<summary> Essential Elements of an Effective PR Description Checklist </summary>

- [X ] The purpose of the PR, such as "Fix some issue (link existing issues this PR will resolve)".
- [X] The test plan, such as providing test command.
- [X] The test results, such as pasting the results comparison before and after, or e2e results
- [ ] (Optional) The necessary documentation update, such as updating `supported_models.md` and `examples` for a new model.
</details>

prs.title: [Build] Update numba version to 0.63.1.
prs.author: nascheme
prs.body_head:
## Purpose

For vLLM to support Python 3.14, we need to use a newer version of numba.  Version 0.63 of numba adds support for 3.14.

## Test Plan

Run ngram_proposer tests using pytest.

## Test Result

The unit tests using ngram_proposer pass.

prs.title: [ROCm][Engine] Fix GPU memory leaks in engine shutdown and test workaround for async KV prefix cache reset
prs.author: AndreasKaratzas
prs.body_head:
When running in-process (`VLLM_ENABLE_V1_MULTIPROCESSING=0`), shutting down the engine leaked GPU memory because:

- **`supports_kw` LRU cache pinned model weights**: The `@lru_cache` on `supports_kw` was keyed on bound methods (e.g. `model.forward`), which held strong references to the model instance and all its GPU tensors for the lifetime of the cache. Fixed by unwrapping bound methods to `__func__` before caching.
- **`gc.freeze()` was never undone**: `EngineCore.__init__` calls `gc.freeze()` to reduce GC overhead during serving, but `shutdown()` never called `gc.unfreeze()`, making all engine objects invisible to the garbage collector.
- **No explicit GPU resource teardown**: `GPUWorker.shutdown()` never freed model weights, KV caches, the rotary embedding cache (`_ROPE_DICT`), compilation contexts, or the workspace singleton.

### Test workaround for prefix cache reset

`reset_prefix_cache` could return `False` indefinitely when async KV store transfers (CPU offloading) were in flight, because the engine stops stepping after all requests finish and never polls the worker for completed transfers. As a temporary workaround (per reviewer suggestion), `_wait_for_prefix_cache_reset` in the test now sends dummy single-token prefills between retries to force the engine to step, which naturally drains completed transfers and frees GPU blocks.

### UCX fix

Bump UCX branch in ROCm Dockerfile to `33b3b2a2` which fixes a memory leak in UCX when used with NIXL.

## Test plan

- [x] `pytest tests/v1/kv_offload/ tests/v1/kv_connector/unit/ tests/v1/core/test_scheduler.py tests/v1/core/test_prefix_caching.py tests/v1/core/test_reset_prefix_cache_e2e.py tests/v1/simple_kv_offload/test_integration.py -v`

### [LEGACY] Initial Test plan and Motivation

- [x] `pytest tests/v1/kv_offload/test_cpu_offloading.py::test_cpu_offloading[TRITON_ATTN-48]`

Motivation: https://buildkite.com/vllm/amd-ci/builds/7062/steps/canvas?sid=019d382d-caab-452a-bf83-1c611ef498b6

prs.title: [ROCm][CI] Fix `trust_remote_code` AttributeError in EAGLE3 acceptance length test
prs.author: AndreasKaratzas
prs.body_head:
Fixes `test_eagle3_acceptance_length` in the `tests/v1/spec_decode` test group (V1 speculative decoding tests). The test's `get_mt_bench_prompts` helper built a `SimpleNamespace` to pass into `vllm.benchmarks.datasets.get_samples`, but omitted `trust_remote_code`. `get_samples` reads `args.trust_remote_code` when constructing the HuggingFace dataset, causing every parametrization of `test_eagle3_acceptance_length` to fail with:

```
AttributeError: 'types.SimpleNamespace' object has no attribute 'trust_remote_code'
```

This patch adds `trust_remote_code=False` to the namespace so the dataset can be constructed as intended.

### Testing

- [x] `pytest tests/v1/spec_decode/test_acceptance_length.py`

cc @kenroche

prs.title: [ROCm][CI] Fix TestSiluMulGroupFp8QuantModel after W8A8 block linear refactor
prs.author: AndreasKaratzas
prs.body_head:
The W8A8 block linear refactor (#33892) replaced `W8A8BlockFp8LinearOp` with `TestFP8Layer` in `TestSiluMulGroupFp8QuantModel` but didn't adapt the forward call: `TestFP8Layer.forward()` takes `(y, bias=None)` while the model was still passing `(y, self.w, self.wscale)`. The redundant weight/scale tensors were also leftover from `W8A8BlockFp8LinearOp` which didn't store its own weights, unlike `TestFP8Layer` which creates them internally.

Additionally, on non-fnuz ROCm (MI355) the fusion pattern expects the triton group quant op, but the kernel's `use_triton` flag was `False` for small test shapes. Fixed by wrapping `quant_fp8` to force `use_triton=True` independently of the gemm path.

cc @kenroche
